title: श्री नन्दी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नन्दी स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमो नन्दिकेश्वराय।
शिववाहनाय वृषभाय नमः॥

नन्दीश्वरं महाभागं शिवभक्तं सनातनम्।
वृषभेशं गणाध्यक्षं प्रणमामि मुदान्वितः॥ १॥

शिवद्वारपालं नन्दिं शिवध्यानपरायणम्।
शिवज्ञानप्रदातारं नन्दिकेशं नमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

श्वेतवर्णं वृषारूढं शूलहस्तं शुभप्रदम्।
नन्दिनं शिवगणेशानं वन्दे भक्तिसमन्वितः॥ ३॥

शिलदमुनिसुतं दिव्यं शिवेन परिपालितम्।
तण्डवे नृत्यतो देवं पश्यन्तं नन्दिनं भजे॥ ४॥

चतुर्वेदविदं नित्यं योगशास्त्रप्रवर्तकम्।
नन्दीश्वरं महायोगं प्रणमामि सदा हृदा॥ ५॥

शिवालयेषु सर्वेषु यः समक्षे विराजते।
शिवदर्शनदातारं नन्दिनं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

कैलासवासिनं नित्यं शिवपार्वतिसेवकम्।
गणानां प्रथमं वन्दे नन्दीश्वरमनामयम्॥ ७॥

नन्दिनं प्रार्थयेद्भक्त्या शिवदर्शनकाङ्क्षया।
नन्दिकृत्यानुमत्या च शिवः प्रसन्नो भवेद् ध्रुवम्॥ ८॥

नन्दिस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
शिवकृपां समाप्नोति शिवलोकं स गच्छति॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री नन्दी स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Nandi Stotram ||

Om Namo Nandikeshwaraya |
Shiva-vahanaya Vrishabhaya namah ||

Nandishwaram Mahabhagam Shiva-bhaktam sanatanam |
Vrishabhesham Gana-adhyaksham pranamami muda-anvitah || 1 ||

Shiva-dvarapalam Nandim Shiva-dhyana-parayanam |
Shiva-jnana-pradataram Nandikesham namamyaham || 2 ||

Shveta-varnam Vrisha-rudham Shula-hastam Shubha-pradam |
Nandinam Shiva-Ganeshanam vande bhakti-samanvitah || 3 ||

Shilada-muni-sutam divyam Shivena paripaalitam |
Tandave nrityato Devam pashyantam Nandinam bhaje || 4 ||

Chatur-veda-vidam nityam Yoga-shastra-pravartakam |
Nandishwaram Maha-yogam pranamami sada hrida || 5 ||

Shivalayeshu sarveshu yah samakshe virajate |
Shiva-darshana-dataram Nandinam pranamamy-aham || 6 ||

Kailasa-vasinam nityam Shiva-Parvati-sevakam |
Gananam prathamam vande Nandishwaram-anamayam || 7 ||

Nandinam prarthayed-bhaktya Shiva-darshana-kankshaya |
Nandi-kritya-anumatya cha Shivah prasanno bhaved dhruvam || 8 ||

Nandi-stotram-idam punyam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah |
Shiva-kripam samapnoti Shivalokam sa gachchhati || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Nandi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: शिवभक्त, सनातन, वृषभेश, गणाध्यक्ष नन्दीश्वर को मैं प्रसन्नतापूर्वक प्रणाम करता हूँ। शिव के द्वारपाल, शिवध्यानपरायण, शिवज्ञान प्रदान करने वाले नन्दिकेश को नमस्कार। श्वेतवर्ण, वृषभ पर आरूढ़, शूलहस्त, शुभप्रद नन्दिन को भक्तिपूर्वक वन्दन। शिलद मुनि के पुत्र, शिव द्वारा पालित, शिव के ताण्डव नृत्य को देखने वाले नन्दिन को भजता हूँ। चतुर्वेदविद, योगशास्त्र के प्रवर्तक, महायोगी नन्दीश्वर को हृदय से प्रणाम। सभी शिवालयों में शिव के सम्मुख विराजमान, शिवदर्शन प्रदान करने वाले नन्दिन को प्रणाम। नन्दी की कृपा और अनुमति से शिव निश्चय ही प्रसन्न होते हैं।
benefits: Pleasing Nandi ensures Lord Shiva's grace
Invokes deep devotion and access to shiva's presence
Invokes the blessing of yogic knowledge and spiritual wisdom
Traditionally invoked for protection from obstacles in shiva worship
Traditionally recited for removal of barriers to spiritual progress
Leads to Shivaloka through Nandi's intercession
description: Nandi Stotram (श्री नन्दी स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Shiva. This revered text originates from Shiva Purana. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove barriers to spiritual progress as well as protect from obstacles in Shiva worship. Additionally, it is known to grant deep devotion and access to Shiva's presence. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Somvar. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Nandi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Nandi Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred prayer to Nandikeshwara, Lord Shiva's divine bull vehicle.
relatedSearches: Why Nandi sits before Shiva temples
Whisper wishes to Nandi Shiva temple

seoFaqs.question: Who is Nandi and why is a stotra dedicated to him?
seoFaqs.answer: Nandi, also called Nandikeshwara or Nandishvara, is the white bull who serves as the vahana (mount) and gatekeeper of Lord Shiva. In the Shiva Purana and Linga Purana, Nandi is far more than a mere vehicle: he is the foremost gana, the chief of Shiva's attendants, and a teacher of the agama traditions. A stotra dedicated to Nandi recognises that no worship of Shiva is considered complete without first paying homage to Nandi, who guards Kailasa and conveys the prayers of devotees to Mahadeva. (Source: Shiva Purana; Linga Purana retellings.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural origin of the Nandi Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Praises of Nandi appear in the Shiva Purana, particularly the Vidyeshvara Samhita and Rudra Samhita, where Nandi is described as the son of sage Shilada, born of penance and granted immortality by Shiva. Several short stotras celebrating Nandi as Vrishabharudha-priya (beloved of the bull-rider) and as Pramathadhipa (lord of the ganas) circulate in temple manuals across South India, especially in Shaiva agamic centres such as Chidambaram, Thiruvannamalai, and Lepakshi. (Source: Shiva Purana Vidyeshvara Samhita; temple agama manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Why do devotees whisper their wishes into Nandi's ear?
seoFaqs.answer: A widely observed temple custom involves devotees cupping their hands and whispering their prayers into the right ear of the Nandi statue facing the sanctum. Tradition teaches that Nandi, being the closest devotee of Shiva and ever in his presence, will personally convey the wish to Mahadeva. This practice is documented at almost every major Shiva temple in India, from Brihadeeswarar in Thanjavur to the small village temples of Kashi. The act symbolises humility, since the devotee approaches the Lord through his servant rather than directly. (Source: Tamil Nadu temple traditions; Shaiva agama practice.)

seoFaqs.question: On which day is the Nandi Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Monday (Somavar) is the principal day for Shiva worship and by extension for Nandi worship. Devotees also recite Nandi Stotram on Pradosha (the thirteenth lunar day of each fortnight), since Pradosha Kaal is sacred to Shiva-Parvati and Nandi is said to dance between the horns of the Lord at that hour. Mahashivaratri, Shravan Mondays, and Karthik Purnima are additional days when temple priests offer special abhisheka and stuti to Nandi before commencing the main Shiva puja. (Source: Drik Panchang; Shaiva temple calendars.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Nandi Stotram offer devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources state that worshipping Nandi cultivates patience, dharmic strength, and unwavering devotion (bhakti) modelled on Nandi's own meditation on Shiva. The stotra is recited to remove obstacles before approaching Shiva, to gain marital harmony (since Nandi witnessed the divine wedding of Shiva and Parvati), to strengthen one's vrata observances, and to receive the grace required for sincere temple worship. Reciting Nandi Stotram before any Shiva mantra japa is considered an essential preliminary in Shaiva tradition. (Source: Shiva Purana; Shaiva agama manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Nandi an avatar of Shiva himself?
seoFaqs.answer: Some Puranic accounts, particularly in the Linga Purana, identify Nandi as a partial manifestation (amsha) of Shiva, while others describe him as Shiva's most beloved devotee elevated to divine status. The Nandikeshwara Kashika, an early treatise on Sanskrit grammar attributed to Nandikeshwara, suggests that Nandi was also a great acharya who received the Maheshwara Sutras directly from Shiva's damaru. Devotees therefore approach Nandi as both servant of Shiva and as Shiva himself in bovine form. (Source: Linga Purana; Nandikeshwara Kashika.)
